chapters.title: Why TVL Exists
chapters.description: Understanding the motivation behind TVL and how it addresses the challenges of governed adaptation in AI pipelines.

sections.title: LLM Pipelines Refuse to Sit Still
sections.content: Large language model development today is notoriously under-specified. Prompts, retrieval depth, model choice, and safety filters often live in ad-hoc notebooks or scattered YAML fragments. TVL addresses the volatility in AI systems where model catalogs shift, latencies spike with traffic bursts, and price lists change without warning.

sections.title: Design Goals
sections.content: TVL is a specification language that captures the semantics needed for safe optimization: type-safe tuning variables, deterministic exports, constraint grammar via CEL, and governed evolution with promotion gates and audit trails.

sections.title: From Hard-Coding to Tuned Variables
sections.content: Instead of manually adjusting configurations during emergencies, TVL lets you express the feasible configuration space, define goals, and let the optimizer search within guardrails. When conditions shift, you rerun the same playbook without rewriting application code.

sections.title: Running Example: Campus Orientation RAG
sections.content: A retrieval-augmented generation assistant that answers questions from incoming students, demonstrating how TVL handles traffic spikes, budget constraints, and latency requirements.
